title: podman hangs with output redirection
body:
/kind bug

This works correctly - shows a list of policies on the screen.
```
✗ podman run --rm -it -v "$(pwd)/.aws":/root/.aws:Z amazon/aws-cli iam list-policies --query "Policies[*].PolicyName"
```
However, redirecting the output make the whole thing hang
```
✗ podman run --rm -it -v "$(pwd)/.aws":/root/.aws:Z amazon/aws-cli iam list-policies --query "Policies[*].PolicyName" > pol
```
The resulting file is there, but incomplete.
```
✗ ls -la pol
-rw-r--r--. 1 anatoli anatoli 1382 Apr 12 10:49 pol
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  git commit: ""

host:

  Distribution:

  IDMappings:

    version: |-
      crun version 0.13
      commit: e79e4de4ac16da0ce48777afb72c6241de870525
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 8174841856
  SwapTotal: 8196714496
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: blackred
  kernel: 5.5.15-200.fc31.x86_64

  slirp4netns:

    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 27h 14m 28.04s (Approximately 1.12 days)

      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.8.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

comments.body:
I think this is caused by allocating a terminal for the container (-t). Try
running without -t and I suspect output redirection will work as expected.

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@mheon yes, removing `-t` key doesn't make it hang.
@mheon is it possible to properly fix it?
I don't believe so - this is how terminal attach works (setting raw mode). Not creating a terminal does not set raw mode and properly splits STDOUT and STDERR so redirection works as expected.
@mheon what could be the point of setting `raw mode` to redirected terminal? If there is no point, maybe in that case `podman` should ignore the flag?

Containers with a terminal are streaming the literal bytes of the
container's terminal and writing them to the target terminal, including
flow control characters, etc; we could look into it, but my initial
impression is that the output would be full of garbage.

On Wed, May 13, 2020 at 8:29 PM Anatoli Babenia <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon> what could be the point of setting raw
